description_long: This bungalow features a single-story structure with a prominent gable roof covered in gray shingles. The front facade includes a covered porch with a transparent or open framework, supported by posts, and a central entry door flanked by windows. The rear of the house showcases a bay window projection, adding architectural interest and natural light to the interior. The exterior walls are finished in a light, neutral color, contrasting with the darker roof and wooden deck that wraps around the building. A pergola-style extension is attached to one side, providing a semi-sheltered outdoor area. The overall design is compact and functional, suitable for suburban or residential neighborhoods.
description: A single-story bungalow with a gray gable roof, covered front porch, bay window at the rear, and a wraparound wooden deck with a pergola extension.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, central entry door, and symmetrical windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the gable roof and the pergola extension on the left.
description_view_2: Rear side view with a plain wall and the wooden deck extending around the house.
description_view_3: Back view featuring a prominent bay window and the pergola structure to the right.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with bay window on rear facade and grouped front windows
